summary: Accepted next-step decision: fix reconciliation lifecycle so accepted builds clear false drift once downstream knowledge, roadmap, implementation, and validation evidence consume them. This should make graph/status next-action trustworthy before more feature work.

accepted_decisions.summary: Prioritize reconciliation lifecycle correctness over new feature work.
Graph should not keep reporting accepted feedback/documentation builds as drift after downstream truth and evidence already consumed them.
Status should become trustworthy: yellow should indicate real action, not stale lifecycle metadata.

assumptions: Existing KB semantics remain: builds are temporary handoffs; graph is generated map/state; roadmap is active work truth.
non_goals: Make graph a source of truth.
Reintroduce deprecated queue files or aliases.

knowledge: Clarify build consumption/lifecycle semantics if needed.
roadmap: Create implementation task for reconciliation lifecycle repair.
code: Update graph reconciliation/build lifecycle logic and tests.
